function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i<5' is never executed?

dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 6:  <statement> for(i=0; i<5; i++) { </statement> is reached, the statement line 8:  <statement> x += i + j - 1; </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 12:  <statement> printf("\nx = %d", x); </statement> dominate line 18:  <statement> printf("%d ", x); </statement>?
data_dependency: Does variable i at line 15: <statement>     for(i=0; i<5; i++) </statement> determine the value stored in variable x at line 17: <statement>             x += i + j - 1; </statement>?
Does the value of variable j at line 16: <statement>         for(j=0; j<i; j++) { </statement> depend on the value of variable x at line 8: <statement>             x += i + j - 1; </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'i' live at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'j' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Is the statement 'x += i + j - 1;' unreachable?
